text.title: VideoPromptBuilder and VideoProcessor
text.summary: Structured prompt construction with VideoPromptBuilder and ffmpeg post-processing utilities.

section.title: VideoPromptBuilder
section.reference: VideoPromptBuilder fluent API with all fields
section.p: VideoPromptBuilder constructs prompts with explicit subject, action, setting, camera, style, and lighting fields. Omitting camera produces random or shaky movements.

rule.statement: Always specify camera movement explicitly in video prompts. Use the defined vocabulary: static, pan_left, pan_right, dolly_in, dolly_out, tracking, crane, drone, orbit.
rule.rationale: Omitting camera movement allows the model to choose randomly — output quality and feel become unpredictable.

example.code: prompt = ( VideoPromptBuilder() .set_subject("a golden retriever") .set_action("running through a field of sunflowers") .set_setting("sunny countryside") .set_camera("tracking") # follows subject .set_style("cinematic") # film quality, 35mm .set_lighting("golden_hour") .add_detail("slow motion") .add_detail("4K quality") .build() ) # → "a golden retriever, running through a field of sunflowers, # in sunny countryside, tracking shot, following subject, # cinematic, film quality, 35mm, golden hour lighting, slow motion, 4K quality"

section.title: VideoProcessor (ffmpeg utilities)
section.reference: ffmpeg concat bash helper for clip sequences
section.p: VideoProcessor wraps ffmpeg for frame extraction, clip concatenation, resize, and audio merging. Always run ffprobe on outputs before downstream processing to catch silent failures (0-byte files).

rule.statement: Run ffprobe validation on every generated video output before passing it to downstream steps. Check file size greater than 0 and valid stream information.
rule.rationale: APIs can return SUCCEEDED status with empty or corrupt output files. Silent downstream failures are harder to debug than upfront validation errors.

rule.statement: Cache generated video clips by prompt hash with seed values. Store seed alongside each clip for near-identical regeneration.
rule.rationale: Video generation is expensive ($0.05-$0.50/clip). Re-generating identical prompts wastes significant budget. Seeds enable minor-variation regeneration.

rule.statement: Keep individual clips under 5s for maximum quality. Chain via ffmpeg concat for longer sequences.
rule.rationale: Current models produce highest quality in their optimal range (5-10s for Runway, 5s for Luma). Longer clips accumulate motion artifacts.

antipattern.description: Attempting to read or stream video content inline in an agent response.
antipattern.reason: Video files are binary and large. Download to disk first; pass the file path to ffprobe/ffmpeg for processing.
